---
title: "About"
---

## What is VedicDateTime?

**VedicDateTime** is an R package for computing entities from the **Vedic (lunisolar) calendar system** and converting between **Gregorian** time and Vedic calendar entities.

The package is described in the open-access paper:

- https://doi.org/10.1007/s11042-023-16553-w

Project links:

- CRAN: https://cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime
- GitHub: https://github.com/neerajdhanraj/VedicDateTime
- Website: https://vedicdatetime.org

## What’s inside (high level)

From the paper, VedicDateTime implements the five key Panchanga entities:

- tithi
- vaara
- nakshatra
- yoga
- karana

…and also provides a broader set of helper functions for related entities such as rashi/lagna/masa/ritu/samvatsara and sunrise/sunset/moonrise/moonset.

## Implementation notes (paper summary)

The paper describes algorithmic design choices including:

- computations anchored to **local sunrise** (location-dependent)
- Lahiri Ayanamsa for sidereal corrections
- use of Swiss Ephemeris (via R dependencies) for astronomical computations
- inverse Lagrange interpolation (with 0.25-day offsets) to compute end times

## License

VedicDateTime is distributed under **GPL-3** (as stated in the paper and in CRAN metadata).

## Funding acknowledgement

Early development of this package was funded through **Google Summer of Code (GSoC)** in multiple years.

## Authors (paper)

- Neeraj Dhanraj Bokde
- Prajwal Kailasnath Patil
- Saradindu Sengupta
- Manisha Sawant
- Andrés E. Feijóo‑Lorenzo

## Limitations & future scope (as stated in the paper)

The paper notes future improvements such as:

- adding **visualization capabilities** for calendar entities
- adding native **R date-time object support** (e.g., S4 classes) for Vedic date-time handling

If you are using VedicDateTime for research, we recommend recording:

- package version
- location & timezone assumptions
- input time conventions
